prose:
**Fukui Reactivity Analysis of Pyridine** using DFT at B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard) in gas phase with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. All three electronic states (neutral, cation N−1, anion N+1) converged successfully at the same level of theory. Mulliken partial charges were computed for the neutral, cation (+1, doublet), and anion (−1, doublet) states, from which Fukui indices were derived: electrophilicity f⁺ (susceptibility to nucleophilic attack), nucleophilicity f⁻ (susceptibility to electrophilic attack), and dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻, net character). 

**Most electrophilic site:** Nitrogen (atom 1, f⁺ = 0.484), highly susceptible to nucleophilic attack, consistent with the ring's electron-withdrawing heteroatom character.

**Most nucleophilic site:** Meta-position carbons (atoms 3–4, f⁻ = 0.118), reflecting the meta-directing effect of nitrogen. Charge conservation checks (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000) confirm numerical integrity. Calculation run with exact four-center integrals (density fitting OFF by default in chemkit). PySCF backend. No warnings reported.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">11
